title: Faloodeh (Rose Water Sorbet)
description: A traditional, elegant Persian dessert featuring thin rice noodles suspended in a semi-frozen, sweet rose water and lime syrup.

instructions.text: In a saucepan, combine the sugar and water. Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring until the sugar is completely dissolved to make a simple syrup.
Remove the syrup from heat and let it cool completely. Once cool, stir in the rose water.
Pour the cooled rose water syrup into a shallow freezer-safe container and place it in the freezer until it becomes slushy (about 2-3 hours). Stir with a fork every 45 minutes.
Break the dry rice vermicelli noodles into 2-inch pieces. Boil them in water according to package directions (about 3-5 minutes) until soft but not mushy.
Drain the noodles and rinse them thoroughly under very cold water to stop the cooking and chill them.
Take the slushy syrup from the freezer, fold in the cold noodles and lime juice. Mix well.
Return the mixture to the freezer for another 1-2 hours until firm but scoopable. Serve in bowls with extra lime juice or sour cherry syrup poured on top.

tips: Make sure you use thin rice vermicelli, not bean threads. Stirring the syrup as it freezes is crucial to prevent a solid block of ice.
storage_notes: Store covered in the freezer for up to 1 month. Let it sit at room temperature for 5-10 minutes to soften before scooping.
serve_with: Bastani Sonnati (Persian Saffron Ice Cream) for a classic 'makhloot' (mixed) dessert.

substitutions.note: Provides a similarly tart and colorful contrast to the sweet rose flavor.
Can be used if limes are unavailable, though lime is more traditional.
